# Tutorial Template (Diátaxis)

<!-- AGENT INSTRUCTIONS: Fill every section below for the specific topic. Remove these comments in the final file. A tutorial is LEARNING-ORIENTED — guide the reader through a hands-on project. Prioritise doing over explaining. -->

---

title: "Tutorial: <Topic Name>"
type: tutorial
audience: all
date: YYYY-MM-DD

---

# Tutorial: <Topic Name>

> **What you'll learn**: One sentence describing the concrete outcome the reader will achieve.
> **Time required**: ~X minutes
> **Prerequisites**: List any required tools, accounts, or prior knowledge.

## Introduction

Two to three sentences: what the reader will build or accomplish, and why it is a useful learning exercise. Keep it motivating and concrete.

## Before you begin

List every prerequisite step the reader must complete before starting:

- Prerequisite 1 (e.g., "The development server is running — see [how-to/run-dev-server.md](../how-to/run-dev-server.md)")
- Prerequisite 2

## Step 1 — \<Action verb + goal\>

One sentence explaining what this step accomplishes and why.

```language
# Concrete code or command the reader runs
```

Expected output or result:

```
Expected output shown here
```

> **Tip**: Optional callout for a common mistake or shortcut.

## Step 2 — \<Action verb + goal\>

Repeat the pattern: explain → code/command → expected result.

## Step 3 — \<Action verb + goal\>

(Add as many steps as needed. Each step should be completable in under 5 minutes.)

## Check your work

Tell the reader how to verify the whole tutorial succeeded. Include a verification command or visible result.

## Summary

Recap in 2–3 sentences what was built and what skills the reader practised.
